learning_outcomes:
1. Major Programme
Upon
completion of the Programme, the graduates will have:
1. demonstrated good knowledge
and understanding of
· anatomy,
biochemistry, pharmacology, physiology, molecular biology, medical genetics,
etc. and selected advanced areas of biomedical sciences (i.e. specialized
topics); · human
diseases – pathophysiology, pathology, diagnosis and genetic counseling; · biomedical
laboratory techniques and related safety procedures; · biostatistics
and research methods; · ethical
and legal issues in biomedical science; · healthcare
system and pharmaceutical management; · legal
perspectives on intellectual property.
2. acquired practical skills to
· perform
a wide range of biomedical laboratory techniques competently; · know
how to make use of a range of modern information technologies; · organize
data and ideas for effective presentations using a variety of formats (e.g.
oral, poster); · work
effectively and cooperatively within a group.
3. acquired cognitive/thinking
skills to
· interpret
and critically evaluate laboratory data; · critically
review scientific literature; · develop
own ideas through critical appraisal and integration of known concepts,
principles and published literature; · design
a research project, collect, analyze, interpret and evaluate research data; recognize,
analyze and respond to the moral, ethical and social issues related to
advances in biomedical science knowledge and research; · recognize
advances and future development in biomedical sciences from local, regional
and global perspectives.
4. acquired skills to
· formulate
a career development plan; · demonstrate
an autonomous and reflective approach to experiential and lifelong learning; · demonstrate
literacy, numeracy and problem-solving skills; · apply
knowledge and skills to career development in education, management,
laboratory-based services, postgraduate studies or research in biomedical
sciences and related clinical or healthcare-related disciplines.
study_scheme:
Biomedical Sciences Biomedical Sciences Applicable to students admitted in 2024-25
Major Programme Requirement
Students
are required to complete a minimum of 70 units of courses as follows:
Units
1.  |  Faculty Package:  |  10
MEDF1011, 1021, 1031, 1041, SBMS1102
2.  |  Required Courses:  |  28
SBMS1101, 1103, 2101, 2102, 2104,
2105, 2106, 2107, 2108, 3101, 3105
3.  |  Elective Courses:
(a)  |  Students must
choose only one Concentration Area from the following:
Concentration
Area A: Biomedical Research[d]
(i)  |  SBMS3201, 4201[e], 4202[e]  |  8
(ii)  |  6 units of
courses chosen from the following:  |  6
SBMS3202, 3203,
3204, 3205, 3206
Concentration Area B: Strategic Management and
Entrepreneurship
(i)  |  EPIN1020#, 2010#, 3020# or 3021# or 3022#, LAWS2331#, SBMS3215,
3216  |  14
(ii)  |  1 course chosen
from the following:  |  1
SBMS4203,
4204
Concentration Area C: Health
Services and Consultation
(i)  |  SBMS3207, 3208, 3209, 3210, 3211  |  13
(ii)  |  1 course chosen from the following:  |  1
SBMS4203, 4204
(b)  |  17-18[a] units of courses chosen from
the following[b]:  |  17-18
EPIN1020#, 2010#, 3020# or 3021# or 3022#, LAWS2331#, PHAR3420#,
SBMS2201, 3201, 3202, 3203, 3204, 3205, 3206, 3207, 3208, 3209, 3210,
3211, 3212, 3213, 3214, 3215, 3216, 4203[c], 4204[c], 4205[c]
Total:  |  70
Explanatory Notes:
1.  |  All
SBMS courses as well as those labeled as # will be included in the
calculation of Major GPA for honours classification.
[a]  |  Students
who choose Concentration Area B are required to take 17 units of elective
courses, while those choosing other Concentration Areas should take 18
units of elective courses.
[b]  |  Courses taken to
fulfill requirement 3(a) cannot be counted towards the fulfillment of
requirement 3(b).
[c]  |  Only for students
choosing Concentration Area B or C.
[d]  |  Concentration Area A students, who
opt for the CUHK-UoM Study Option and have successfully completed 180
credits of modules in Master of Science in Infection Biology or Master of
Science in Tissue Engineering for Regenerative Medicine offered by the Faculty
of Biology, Medicine and Health, The University of Manchester, will be
granted course and unit exemptions for up to 18 units of Major Elective
Courses.
